name: Black Bean Sauce Squid
recipeIngredient: 2 tbsp Fermented, salted black beans (available at Asian groceries)
2 tbsp Rice wine or mirin or sherry
1 tsp cornstarch dissolved in 1 tbsp water
recipeInstructions:
1. Many people shy away from dishes with names like Black Bean Sauce Squid because they think it involves the preparation of a complicated sauce. In fact, it is quite easy. The black beans themselves, fermented and salted when you buy them, are the sauce. All you do is add a little rice wine to make a paste.
2. Mix together: 2 tbsp rice wine, 1 tbsp soy sauce, and 1 tsp sugar in a small bowl. Let sit for 15 minutes.
3. Heat vegetable oil in a heavy frying pan or wok over medium-high heat. Add garlic and ginger; sauté until garlic is lightly browned.
4. Increase heat to high. Add squid and scallions; stir-fry for 30 seconds.
5. Pour in the black bean sauce mixture, stirring continuously. Cook for 2 minutes.
6. Stir in cornstarch (dissolved in water). Continue cooking until the sauce thickens. Remove from heat and serve.
Slicing:
Score squid diagonally at three-quarter inch intervals. Turn and repeat at right angles to original cuts. Do not cut all the way through the squid body. Cut each scored squid body into three or four pieces. When cooked, the squid will roll up and the crosshatching will catch and hold the sauce. It's much easier to do than to describe.
